body_txt: God (or suitable equivalent thereof ) works in mysterious ways. How mysterious? Really mysterious. Like, chaos theory mysterious. Who knew that ending a war could be as easy as helping one little old lady cross the street — or letting the Girl of the Week get run over by a truck ? In Mysterious Ways is what happens when the divine interacts with the mundane in ways that don't make sense at first appearance. In time, however, the results are palpable. This is usually done to convey a sense of omniscience; after all, if you can see how all events play out, it's more likely you'd go the subtle route than try to rain fire from Heaven every time things don't go your way . Characters who fall under this trope are unlikely to use an Omniscient Morality License ; why put the hero through hell when you can save the day by moving a book across the room? Unless that was the only way they could grow as a person ... If God Is Inept or merely Flawed , He might try to claim " I Meant to Do That !" by invoking this trope. Implies Butterfly of Doom . Usually overlaps with Gambit Roulette , which is about plans relying on seemingly chance events in general. Compare Maybe Magic, Maybe Mundane and Butterfly of Doom . If there are two or more divine players trying to influence events at the same time, it may be a Cosmic Chess Game . Often used as flimsy justification for Deus ex Machina and A Wizard Did It . Parodies may overlap with Missing Steps Plan . Contrast Divine Intervention , when it's very obvious that the deity was taking action. Compare and contrast Answer to Prayers , when the deity gets involved because their followers asked; it's an open question how clear their involvement will be. Not to be confused with the U2 song "Mysterious Ways" (that would be God-Is-Love Songs ).
